content.full_text:
State of California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O BOX 907 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

February 24, 1978


Mr. David S. Kershaw
2215 Whyte Park Ave.
Walnut Creek, California  94595

Dear Mr. Kershaw:

     Thank you for your letter dated February 5, 1978. In your letter you have asked whether Gustav Dorcough, Walter Newel and Robert Lee, employees of Lawrence Livermore Laboratory, have any vested interests in Control Data Corp. or Cray Research Incorporated.

     On January 26, 1978, the Fair Political Practices Commission approved the Conflict of Interest Code for the University of California. The Code does not become effective for the University (except for the Regents) until March 27, 1978. Thirty days after the effective date of the Code April 26, 1978, University employees designated in the Code must file initial statements, disclosing their interests in real property and investments with the University's filing officer.

     Given that statements disclosing financial interests need not be filed until April 26, 1978, it is impossible for our office to respond to the questions put forth in your letter at this time. It should be pointed out that when the statements are filed by the April 26, 1978 deadline, the University's filing officer will maintain copies for public inspection.

     I hope this information has been helpful. If we can be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact our office.

                                                 Sincerely,


                                                 Dwight Dickerson
                                                 Staff Counsel
                                                 Conflicts of Interest Division

2315 Whyte Park Avenue

Walnut Creek, California 94595

February 5,1978


State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

1100 K Street Building

Sacramento, California 95814


Dear Sir:

I am a physicist at the University of California,

Lawrence Livermore Laboratory and I am concerned about

the future computational capability of the laboratory.

Some far reaching decisions on large research computer

acquisitions are now being made and I would like to know

if the people making these decisions have any vested

interest in the companies involved. Specifically I would

like to know if

     Gustav Dorough - Associate Director for Computations

     Walter Neryick - Dorough's staff

     Robert Lee     - Head of Computations Division

own any stock in

     Control Data Corporation - N.Y.S.E.

     Cray Research Incorporated - C.T.C.  ,

the vendors whose computers are being considered for

acquisition by Lawrence Livermore Laboratory.

     I would like to know how much stock they held (if any)

and (if this information is available) how much stock

their wives held since under California community property

law this would be half theirs.

     Time is of the essence since these bids, contracts and

(2)

performance evaluation tests are being written and sent
out right now.

I would appreciate it if my name and request were
kept confidential as I would not like it known around
the laboratory that I have been checking into this.

Sincerely yours,

David S. Kershaw
David S. Kershaw
